prompt:
Add Ollama with CUDA support and Open WebUI to classic-laddie for local LLM inference.

## Context
classic-laddie has an NVIDIA RTX 4090 with full driver support already configured (hardware.nix has nvidia drivers, modesetting, video/render groups). We want to run Gemma 4 26B MoE locally.

## Changes needed

### 1. Add Ollama service to hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix

Add an Ollama section following the existing service pattern (see github-relay, reel-life sections for style):

```nix
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ollama (local LLM inference)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
services.ollama = {
  enable = true;
  acceleration = "cuda";
  loadModels = [ "gemma4:26b" ];
};
```

### 2. Add Open WebUI for a chat interface

Check if `services.open-webui` is available in nixpkgs. If it is, add:

```nix
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open WebUI (LLM chat interface)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
services.open-webui = {
  enable = true;
  openFirewall = true;
};
```

If the NixOS module isn't available, skip it and note in the PR that it can be added later.

### 3. Firewall considerations

Ollama should NOT be exposed on the firewall (only localhost access). Open WebUI should be accessible on the LAN.

## Style guide
- Follow the existing section comment pattern with dashed separator lines
- Place the new sections logically near other services (after the github-relay block is fine)
- Keep it minimal — don't over-configure

## Constraints
- Do NOT modify hardware.nix — GPU drivers are already configured
- Do NOT run nixos-rebuild
- Check that the NixOS options you use actually exist: run `nix eval .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.options.services.ollama.enable 2>&1 | head -5` or similar to verify the module is available before committing
